title: Coffee Can Investing
author: Saurabh Mukherjea
subtitle: The Low-Risk Route to Super-High Returns
description: A practical guide to building long-term wealth through high-quality Indian stocks, co-authored by Saurabh Mukherjea, Rakshit Ranjan, and Pranab Uniyal. The book adapts the classic 'Coffee Can' portfolio concept — buying quality stocks and holding them for decades — to the Indian market. It introduces the 'Coffee Can' screen: seven simple filters that identify companies with sustainable competitive advantages, strong governance, and consistent performance.
keyIdeas: Buy high-quality companies and hold them for decades — the coffee can approach
Seven-filter screen identifies companies with sustainable competitive advantage
Return on Capital Employed (ROCE) is the single most important quality metric
Cash flow from operations must consistently support reported earnings
Promoter holding and governance are critical filters for Indian stocks
Compounding works best when you let winners run for 10-20 years
Ignore macroeconomic noise — focus on company quality
Small STIRs (Stop-losses) can kill a coffee can portfolio
A portfolio of 15-20 high-quality stocks provides sufficient diversification
India's economic growth makes it a fertile market for coffee can investing
keyTakeaways: A Coffee Can portfolio — buy quality stocks and hold for decades — is the simplest path to wealth
ROCE (Return on Capital Employed) above 15% for 10+ years is the strongest quality signal
Promoter (founder/family) holding above 50-60% indicates alignment with minority shareholders
Seven filters eliminate 99% of listed Indian stocks — leaving only the best
Ignore interest rates, GDP growth, elections, and other macro noise
Let winners compound — selling early is the biggest mistake
Dividends from quality companies provide income without selling shares
A coffee can portfolio can generate 15-18% CAGR over long periods
India's demographic dividend supports long-term equity returns
whoShouldRead: Indian retail investors looking for a systematic, low-risk investing approach
Long-term investors tired of active trading and looking for a buy-and-hold strategy
Anyone who wants a simple, evidence-based stock selection framework
Global investors interested in the Indian equity market
